page_summary: A page containing a constructed/runic script text in two paragraphs, with a large stylized Chinese oracle-bone-style glyph at the bottom of the page.

symbols.description: A large black glyph at the bottom of the page consisting of a horizontal top stroke, a vertical line descending from its center, and a triangular/wedge-shaped body with two long outward-sweeping legs at the bottom, with a small inner triangle where the legs meet. It strongly resembles a Chinese character in oracle-bone or seal script form, possibly 天 (tian, 'heaven/sky') or 大 (da, 'big/great') rendered in a stylized, slightly asymmetrical hand.
symbols.position: bottom center of page, below the two text blocks
symbols.size_estimate: very large (occupies roughly the lower quarter of the page)

uncertain_or_unreadable: The text is written in a constructed or fictional script (resembles the script used in 'The Wolhaiksong' or a similar invented alphabet); the individual characters cannot be confidently transcribed as a real language.
I transcribed the text by rendering placeholder shapes; these are NOT a real reading of the page and should not be treated as the actual content.
The precise identity of the bottom glyph (whether it is intended as 天, 大, or a custom invented logograph) cannot be determined with certainty from the image alone.
